JavaScript Core Concepts (8): How Memory Gets Allocated and Freed (Garbage Collection)

Let's talk about how JavaScript reclaims memory. This post covers what garbage collection actually is, why older languages made you manage memory by hand, the rule JavaScript uses to decide when an object is safe to collect, and a hands-on Chrome experiment to watch memory get allocated and freed in real time.

The 7-1 pattern is about organising your directory structure. Back when you had to dump all your CSS in one place, maintenance was painful. Sass lets you use `@import` plus folder organisation — that's the 7-1 pattern.
How to Add ESLint to Vue CLI
Most projects add a code style manager (ESLint) to keep code quality consistent — but how should you actually add it to a Vue CLI project?
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (15) - Collision Detection with Bump

Last time we learned how to draw geometric shapes in PIXI. Now it's time for something I find pretty interesting — collision detection. If you've ever Googled "PIXI hitTestRectangle" and hit a wall, this post explains why, and shows you a couple of ready-to-use functions you can drop straight into your project.
BEM is a CSS design pattern that makes class architecture easier to maintain. The main downside is overly long class names — but plenty of real projects still use it.
OOCSS is another CSS design pattern. The most prominent framework using it is Bootstrap.
CSS design patterns are concepts, conventions, and ways of thinking that improve dev and maintenance efficiency. Let's start with SMACSS.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (14) - Graphics (2): Circles, Polygons, Lines and Stars

Last time we covered basic geometry drawing in PixiJS, but only the rectangle. PIXI actually gives you a bunch of other shapes to draw too — circles, ellipses, rounded rectangles, polygons, lines and even stars. So this post walks through all the other geometry methods one by one.
Windows "running scripts is disabled on this system" — How to Fix PowerShell Execution Policy

Hit "running scripts is disabled on this system, so cannot load..." in Windows PowerShell? Windows defaults the Execution Policy to Restricted (especially on Windows 10 PowerShell), which blocks script execution as a security measure. This post explains the Execution Policy levels (AllSigned, Bypass, Default, RemoteSigned, Restricted, Undefined, Unrestricted) and how to switch to RemoteSigned so npm, vue, gulp, webpack, etc. work normally.
JavaScript Core Concepts (7): not defined VS undefined — What's the Difference?

not defined and undefined are two really important concepts in JavaScript, and they trip people up all the time. Once you understand the difference between them, debugging gets a whole lot easier. This post walks through what each one actually means, and why you should never use undefined as a value or a variable name.
